sections.paragraph:
to lay up, to store, to heap up, to treasure up. (The primary idea is that of shutting up, enclosing, restraining; compare the cognate roots חָצַר, עָצַר, also אָזַר, אָסַר and Arab. أَصَرَ to shut up, to restrain, kindred to which are اسر and حصر.) 2Ki 20:17 Isa 39:6 Amo 3:10.

Niphal, pass. Isa 23:18.

Hiphil, to cause to lay up in a treasury, i.e. to set any one over a treasury; Neh 13:13, וָאֹוצְרָה עַל אֹוצָרֹות “and I made treasurers over the treasuries.”

Hence are derived אֹוצָר and-
